ddls:
CREATE TABLE "research_sites" (
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each research site */,
  "code" TEXT /* Code assigned to the site */,
  "site_name" TEXT /* Name of the site */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the site */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude coordinate of the site */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude coordinate of the site */,
  "area" REAL /* Area of the site in square kilometers */,
  "site_type" TEXT /* Type of research site (e.g., forest, grassland, tundra) */,
  "established_date" TEXT /* Date when the site was established */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the site */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "site_characteristics" (
  "characteristic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each site characteristic entry */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the site the characteristic is related to */,
  "vegetation" TEXT /* Type of vegetation found at the site */,
  "topography" TEXT /* Description of the site's topography */,
  "soil_type" TEXT /* Type of soil found at the site */,
  "water_source" TEXT /* Source of water at the site (e.g., river, lake, rain) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("characteristic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_site_characteristics_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES research_sites ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "environmental_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the site where the data was collected */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature recorded at the site in degrees Celsius */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity percentage recorded at the site */,
  "precipitation" REAL /* Precipitation measured at the site in millimeters */,
  "date_collected" TEXT /* Date when the data was collected */,
  "instrument_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instrument used to collect the data */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data (e.g., good, suspect, invalid) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_environmental_data_instrument_id FOREIGN KEY ("instrument_id") REFERENCES instruments ("instrument_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_environmental_data_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES research_sites ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instruments" (
  "instrument_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instrument */,
  "instrument_name" TEXT /* Name of the instrument */,
  "instrument_type" TEXT /* Type of instrument (e.g., thermometer, hygrometer) */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date when the instrument was last calibrated */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the instrument */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("instrument_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "species_observations" (
  "observation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each observation entry */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the site where the observation was made */,
  "species_name" TEXT /* Name of the species observed */,
  "observation_date" TEXT /* Date when the observation was made */,
  "abundance" INTEGER /* Abundance of the species observed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("observation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_species_observations_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES research_sites ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the site being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the site was accessed */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action taken (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "data_accessed" TEXT /* Specific data accessed (e.g., temperature, species observations) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES research_sites ("site_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "site_photos" (
  "photo_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each photo entry */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the site the photo is related to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path of the stored photo */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the photo was uploaded */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the photo */,
  "photographer" TEXT /* Name of the photographer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("photo_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_site_photos_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES research_sites ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "site_visits" (
  "visit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each visit record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the visit */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the site visited */,
  "visit_date" TEXT /* Date of the visit */,
  "purpose" TEXT /* Purpose of the visit (e.g., research, monitoring) */,
  "findings" TEXT /* Findings or notes from the visit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("visit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_site_visits_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES research_sites ("site_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_site_visits_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "public_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each public data entry */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the site the data is related to */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data (e.g., temperature, species observations) */,
  "data_value" TEXT /* Value of the data */,
  "date_published" TEXT /* Date when the data was published */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_public_data_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES research_sites ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "site_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the site the report is related to */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., annual, seasonal) */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "report_file" TEXT /* File path of the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_site_reports_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES research_sites ("site_id")
)
